name: QuickClip Organize

releases.author: vipsyvipul
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
  - Fixed crash on fresh vaults — plugin no longer throws TypeError when opening the dashboard in a vault where clipped
  files don't exist
  - Stale entries whose associated note has been deleted are now removed from the dashboard instead of falling back to
  opening the source URL
  - Updated empty state message to be plugin-focused

releases.author: vipsyvipul
releases.description:
- Replace builtin-modules with Node.js built-in module API
- Remove !important declarations from CSS using higher specificity selectors

releases.author: vipsyvipul
releases.description:
## QuickClip Organize — Initial Release

  A Portent-based dashboard to classify, connect, and track every note through its lifecycle — from raw capture to
  organized knowledge.

  ### What's included                                                                            

  - **Dashboard** — native Obsidian view with All Clips, By Domain, By Type, and Archived tabs
  - **Portent type system** — classify any note as one of 8 types (Project, Operation, Responsibility, Task, Event,
  Note, Topic, Person)
  - **Belongs To / Related To** — wikilink chip fields to connect notes to projects and related content
  - **Progress tracking** — three-state indicator (Raw → Planning → Organized) derived automatically from each entry's
  state
  - **Archive** — hide completed entries from active views without deleting them
  - **Filters** — Type, Progress, Content Type, Date — persistent across sessions
  - **Column management** — show/hide and drag-to-reorder columns
  - **Works standalone** — any note with Portent frontmatter appears automatically; no extension required
  - **Enhanced with QuickClip** — highlight-level web captures surface with auto-filled metadata
